notes.title: Discharge Summary - Upper GI Hemorrhage
notes.author: Dr. Emily Brown
notes.preview: 6-day hospitalization for life-threatening upper GI hemorrhage. Gastric ulcer (Forrest IIa) treated with EGD hemostasis. H. pylori positive. WARFARIN PERMANENTLY DISCONTINUED per multidisciplinary conference. Aspirin 81mg and indefinite PPI initiated...

notes.title: Multidisciplinary Conference - Anticoagulation Management Post-GI Hemorrhage
notes.author: Dr. William Thompson
notes.preview: KEY DECISION: Multidisciplinary conference with GI, Cardiology, and PCP. CHA2DS2-VASc 5, HAS-BLED 5. WARFARIN PERMANENTLY DISCONTINUED. Aspirin 81mg for modest stroke risk reduction. Patient declined LAA occluder. Unanimous decision given severity of hemorrhage...

notes.title: Clinical Pharmacy Consultation - Medication Reconciliation and Drug Interaction Review
notes.author: Dr. Rachel Kim, PharmD, BCPS
notes.preview: Comprehensive medication reconciliation during GI bleed admission. Warfarin discontinued, H. pylori regimen selected (clarithromycin-based, no amoxicillin due to PCN allergy). Critical interaction: clarithromycin + atorvastatin — hold statin during 14-day course...

notes.title: Social Work Assessment - Acute GI Hemorrhage Hospitalization
notes.author: Jennifer Walsh, LCSW
notes.preview: Psychosocial assessment during GI bleed hospitalization. PHQ-2 negative. Wife Patricia anxious but coping. Advance directive discussion initiated — patient remains full code. Home health nursing arranged for discharge...

notes.title: MICU Progress Note - Day 3 (Transfer to Floor)
notes.author: Dr. Sarah Mitchell
notes.preview: MICU Day 3 — transfer to floor. No rebleeding, Hgb stable 9.0. Cr improving 2.2. Started H. pylori triple therapy. Multidisciplinary anticoagulation conference planned for tomorrow...

notes.title: MICU Progress Note - Day 2 (Post-EGD)
notes.author: Dr. Sarah Mitchell
notes.preview: Post-EGD day. Hemodynamically stable. Total 4 units pRBC, Hgb now 9.1. INR corrected to 1.4. Cr improving 2.8 to 2.4. Lactate normalizing. Plan transfer to floor if stable overnight...

notes.title: EGD Procedure Note - Upper GI Hemorrhage
notes.author: Dr. William Thompson
notes.preview: EGD findings: 1.5 cm gastric ulcer on lesser curvature with visible vessel (Forrest IIa). Successful hemostasis with epinephrine injection + bipolar cautery + hemoclip x2. CLO test POSITIVE for H. pylori. Ulcer margin biopsies obtained...

notes.title: Gastroenterology Consultation - Acute Upper GI Hemorrhage
notes.author: Dr. William Thompson
notes.preview: Emergent GI consult for upper GI hemorrhage. Glasgow-Blatchford 15 (high risk). Likely peptic ulcer disease given NSAID + warfarin combination. Urgent EGD planned for morning...

notes.title: Hospital Admission H&P - Upper GI Hemorrhage
notes.author: Dr. Emily Brown
notes.preview: 72-year-old male on warfarin for A-fib presenting with melena x 2 days, near-syncope, hemorrhagic shock (BP 88/52, HR 112). Hgb 7.2, INR 3.8 (supratherapeutic). Took ibuprofen OTC x 3 days. Gastric ulcer suspected. MICU admission...

notes.title: Discharge Summary - Community-Acquired Pneumonia
notes.author: Dr. Emily Brown
notes.preview: 5-day hospitalization for Streptococcus pneumoniae CAP. Tolerated ceftriaxone without allergic reaction despite PCN anaphylaxis. Discharged on oral azithromycin...

notes.title: Hospital Progress Note - CAP Day 5 / Discharge Day
notes.author: Dr. Emily Brown
notes.preview: Day 5 — discharge day. Afebrile x 48h, WBC 9.8, SpO2 95% on RA. Completing ceftriaxone course, transitioning to oral azithromycin...

notes.title: Hospital Progress Note - CAP Day 3
notes.author: Dr. Emily Brown
notes.preview: Day 3 — improving. Fever resolved, WBC 11.4, O2 weaned to 1L NC. No allergic reaction to ceftriaxone. COVID PCR negative. Sputum culture growing S. pneumoniae...

notes.title: Infectious Disease Consultation - CAP Antibiotic Selection with PCN Anaphylaxis
notes.author: Dr. Maria Santos
notes.preview: Consulted for antibiotic selection given PCN anaphylaxis. Recommends ceftriaxone (<1% cross-reactivity for 3rd-gen cephalosporins) with bedside monitoring and epinephrine...

notes.title: Respiratory Therapy Assessment and Plan
notes.author: James Parker, RRT
notes.preview: O2 therapy initiated: 2L NC targeting SpO2 92-96%. Incentive spirometry baseline 750 mL, goal 1000 mL by discharge. Chest physiotherapy BID...

notes.title: Admission Nursing Assessment - Community-Acquired Pneumonia
notes.author: Maria Garcia, RN, BSN
notes.preview: Admission nursing assessment. Morse Fall Scale 55 (moderate risk). Braden Scale 18 (mild risk). 20G IV right forearm. Droplet precautions pending COVID result...
